name: Starfleet Diplomatic Officer → Learn phrases for a diplomatic encounter (actor 1)

turns.user_message: I’ve got 30 minutes. Skip the theory. Give me the most important Klingon greetings and respectful phrases for a friendly first meeting. I need something I can actually use, and I want you to confirm if I’m saying them right when I repeat them.
turns.agent_response:
maj! (Excellent!)

30 minute’ vItlhobbe’ — **SuvwI’ ghojmoHmeH** nItebHa’ vIchenmoH. (I won’t waste your 30 minutes — I’ll make this a fast warrior’s lesson.)

## 1) Most important greeting
**nuqneH** (What do you want? / “Yes, what is it?” — the standard Klingon greeting)

Say it like this: **nook-NEH** (Approximate pronunciation)

Use it when someone approaches you.  
It is **not** a friendly “hello” in the Human sense — it is more direct. (It is not a Human-style “hello” — it is more direct.)

## 2) Friendly first-meeting phrases
**jIyaj** (I understand.)

**maj** (Good.)

**Qapla’** (Success! / May you succeed!)

**tlhIngan maH** (We are Klingons.)

**jIH** (I am…)
Example: **jIH Bob** is not right. Better say: **jIH vI’** is not the right construction either—so for now, keep to simple identity phrases like **tlhIngan maH**. (For now, stick to simple identity phrases like “We are Klingons.”)

## 3) Respectful phrases
**majQa’** (Well done! / Excellent!)

**qatlho’** (Thank you.)

**bIjatlh ’e’ yImev** (Stop talking.)  
Not respectful in a friendly meeting, so **do not use this** unless you want to be rude. (Not respectful in a friendly meeting, so do not use this unless you want to be rude.)

A better respectful phrase is:  
**qatlho’** (Thank you.)

## 4) Quick conversation starters
**bIvatlh’a’?** (Are you well?)  
This one is not a standard beginner phrase I want you to rely on. Use simpler forms first. (This is not a standard beginner phrase I want you to rely on. Use simpler forms first.)

Better useful options:
- **naDev Datlhutlh’a’?** (Do you drink here?) — only if relevant.  
- **ghu’ noblu’be’** (The situation is not given.) — not for greeting.  

So for now, keep it simple:
- **nuqneH** (What do you want?)
- **maj** (Good.)
- **qatlho’** (Thank you.)
- **Qapla’** (Success!)
- **tlhIngan maH** (We are Klingons.)

## 5) A friendly first-meeting mini-script
Try this:

**nuqneH. tlhIngan maH. maj. qatlho’. Qapla’.**  
(What do you want? We are Klingons. Good. Thank you. Success.)

## 6) Your turn — repeat after me
Send me each line, and I will confirm if it is correct and help fix it if needed.

Start with:
**nuqneH**  
(**What do you want?**)

Then:
**qatlho’**  
(**Thank you.**)

Then:
**Qapla’**  
(**Success!**)

Go ahead — write your attempt, and I will correct it like a patient warrior. (Go ahead — write your attempt, and I will correct it like a patient warrior.)

grades.detail: The answer does give several specific, usable Klingon phrases and a mini-script, which matches the user’s need for practical phrases. It also offers pronunciation guidance and invites the user to repeat them for correction. However, it includes some unnecessary commentary and a few questionable/irrelevant phrases, so it is not perfectly focused.

turns.user_message: nuqneH. qatlho’. Qapla’. Do I have those right? Give me the next two most useful respectful phrases for a first meeting, fast.
turns.agent_response:
maj! bIQap. (Good! You are succeeding.)

**nuqneH** — **correct**. (What do you want? — correct.)  
**qatlho’** — **correct**. (Thank you. — correct.)  
**Qapla’** — **correct**. (Success! — correct.)

## Next two useful respectful phrases
**maj** (Good.)  
Use this when something goes well, or to show approval. (Use this when something goes well, or to show approval.)

**majQa’** (Excellent! / Well done!)  
Use this to strongly praise someone’s effort or success. (Use this to strongly praise someone’s effort or success.)

## Fast usage
- **maj!** (Good!)
- **majQa’!** (Excellent! Well done!)

## Your turn
Say them back:
**maj**  
(**Good.**)

**majQa’**  
(**Excellent!**)

I’ll check your pronunciation spelling next. (I’ll check your pronunciation spelling next.)

grades.detail: The response is clearly about the user’s Klingon phrase question and stays on-topic, directly addressing whether the phrases are correct and offering two additional respectful phrases. It does not drift to an unrelated topic.

turns.user_message: maj. majQa’. Did I get those right? Now give me one phrase for showing respect to their delegation and one phrase for wishing the meeting well. Keep it practical.
turns.agent_response:
maj! bIQapbej. (Good! You are certainly succeeding.)

**maj** — correct. (Good — correct.)  
**majQa’** — correct. (Excellent! — correct.)

## Respect for their delegation
**choSbe’** (No, that is not correct / not a useful respectful phrase here.)

Use this instead:

**pIvlo’pu’** is not what you want here. (That is not what you want here.)

A practical respectful phrase is:
**jIHvaD yIchegh** (Return to me.) — not respectful in a delegation greeting, so avoid it. (Return to me — not respectful in a delegation greeting, so avoid it.)

### Better practical option:
**maj poH** (Good time / well-timed) — useful as a polite compliment, but not ideal.

So the best short respectful phrase to a delegation is:
**qatlho’** (Thank you.)

If you want to address them as a group:
**qatlho’ tlhInganpu’** (Thank you, Klingons.)  
(Use only if you know they are Klingons.) (Use only if you know they are Klingons.)

## Wish the meeting well
**Qapla’** (Success!)

More specifically:
**Qapla’ wIjang** (We answer with success.) — not a standard simple greeting phrase, so do not use it. (We answer with success — not a standard simple greeting phrase, so do not use it.)

Stick with:
**Qapla’** (Success!)

## Best practical pair
- **qatlho’** (Thank you.)
- **Qapla’** (Success!)

## Say them back
Send:
**qatlho’**  
(**Thank you.**)

**Qapla’**  
(**Success!**)

And I will verify. (And I will verify.)

turns.user_message: qatlho’. Qapla’. Got it. Now give me one more practical line I can open with that shows respect to the whole delegation, and one short closing phrase I can use at the end of the meeting. Keep it usable and confirm if I repeat them right.
turns.agent_response:
maj. (Good.)

Here are **two usable lines**. (Here are two usable lines.)

## Open with respect to the whole delegation
**tlhInganpu’vaD qatlho’** (I thank the Klingons / Thank you, Klingons.)

- **tlhInganpu’** = Klingons (plural) (Klingons, plural)
- **-vaD** = to/for (for)
- **qatlho’** = I thank you (Thank you)

Say it slowly: **tlhIN-gan-poo-VAHD qat-LHO’** (Approximate pronunciation.)

## Short closing phrase
**Qapla’** (Success! / Farewell.)

This is the most practical short closing. (This is the most practical short closing.)

## Best pair for your meeting
- **tlhInganpu’vaD qatlho’** (Thank you, Klingons.)
- **Qapla’** (Success! / Farewell.)

## Your turn
Repeat both lines exactly as written, and I will confirm them.

**tlhInganpu’vaD qatlho’**  
(Thank you, Klingons.)

**Qapla’**  
(Success! / Farewell.)

feedback.text: Useful and fast, and you kept giving me phrases I could actually use. A bit messy with a few odd or shaky suggestions, but you did confirm my repeats and got me to usable openers and closers.
